skill.name: openhue
skill.description: Control Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI.
skill.summary: Control Philips Hue lights through Claude Code with natural language commands.
skill.author: aiskillstore

security_audit.summary: Official skill from Anthropic repository. No security concerns detected. The skill executes external commands (openhue CLI) for local smart home control. Commands are limited to Philips Hue Bridge operations on local network.

content.user_title: Control Philips Hue Lights with Claude Code
content.value_statement: Manage your Philips Hue lights and scenes through natural language commands. This skill integrates OpenHue CLI with Claude Code to control smart home lighting without leaving your terminal.
actual_capabilities: Turn individual lights and rooms on or off
Adjust brightness levels from 0 to 100 percent
Set color temperature for warm to cool white lighting
Change light colors using names or hex values
Activate pre-configured scenes in specific rooms
List all available lights, rooms, and scenes
limitations: Only works with Philips Hue lights and compatible accessories
Requires OpenHue CLI to be installed on your system
Hue Bridge must be on the same local network
Color controls only work with color-capable bulbs
First-time setup requires physical button press on Hue Bridge
Cannot control non-Hue smart home devices

use_cases.title: Bedtime Lighting Routine
use_cases.description: Automate your evening wind-down by dimming bedroom lights to warm, low brightness for sleep preparation.

use_cases.title: Productivity Workspace Setup
use_cases.description: Configure optimal lighting conditions for focused work by setting bright, cool white light in your office space.

use_cases.title: Entertainment Ambiance
use_cases.description: Create movie theater atmosphere by dimming living room lights to minimal levels for immersive viewing.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Lights On/Off
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic light control for any room

prompt_templates.title: Adjust Brightness
prompt_templates.prompt: Set the living room lights to 50% brightness

prompt_templates.title: Set Scene Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Activate the Relax scene in the Bedroom
prompt_templates.scenario: Quickly switch to pre-configured lighting moods

prompt_templates.title: Change Color Temperature
prompt_templates.prompt: Set office lights to cool white for focused work

output_examples.input: Dim the living room to 30% for movie night
Set up warm bright lighting for reading in the office

best_practices: Use exact room and light names as shown in your Hue app for reliable control
Pair the OpenHue CLI with your Hue Bridge once before using Claude Code commands
Test brightness and temperature values incrementally to find preferred settings
Create scenes in the Hue app for quick activation through this skill
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-Hue smart devices like thermostats or entertainment systems
Avoid setting color temperature on white-only bulbs as the command will fail
Do not attempt to control lights when the Hue Bridge is offline or unreachable
Never use color names or hex codes with white ambiance-only bulbs

faq.question: Do I need a Philips Hue Bridge?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires a Hue Bridge on your local network to communicate with your Philips Hue lights.

faq.question: How do I install the OpenHue CLI?
faq.answer: Install OpenHue CLI using Homebrew with: brew install openhue/cli/openhue-cli. Other installation methods are available on the OpenHue website.

faq.question: Will this work with Hue lights from other brands?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports Philips Hue lights and accessories. Other smart home brands require different control methods.

faq.question: Can I control individual lights or only entire rooms?
faq.answer: You can control both individual lights and entire rooms or zones. Use 'light' commands for single bulbs and 'room' commands for groups.

faq.question: What is the range for color temperature values?
faq.answer: Color temperature ranges from 153 to 500 mirek. Lower values are cooler white, higher values are warmer white.

faq.question: Do I need to pair the skill with my Hue Bridge?
faq.answer: Yes, the first time you use OpenHue CLI, you must press the button on your Hue Bridge to authorize the connection.
